channel.title: Us Politics on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Us Politics on MinWeb

item.title: What Is the 'Mamdani Act'? Inside the GOP’s New Bill
item.description:
If you follow politics, you’ve noticed the trend: proposals are getting bolder and honestly, a bit surreal. Just when you think you’ve seen the absolute ceiling of legislative ambition, a new bill drops that forces even the most cynical junkies to pause and ask, “Is this actually possible?”

That’s the exact reaction to the “Mamdani Act.” Introduced on April 20, 2026, by Representative Chip Roy (R-TX), the bill has sent shockwaves through Washington. It attempts to fundamentally rewrite how the U.S. treats immigrants, citizens, and political ideas. But what exactly is it, why is it called that, and why are legal experts already betting it will be DOA in court?

item.title: Trump and Joe Rogan’s New Executive Order on Psychedelics
item.description:
If you’ve been scrolling through your social media since April 18, 2026, you’ve seen it: Donald Trump at the Resolute Desk, pen in hand, with Joe Rogan hovering over his shoulder. The optics were loud, the timing was calculated, and the internet exploded.

For a lot of people, this photo feels like a collision of two worlds that were just at war. Only months ago, Rogan was tearing into the administration’s involvement in Iran and fighting the federal hemp ban. Now, they’re partners in a major policy pivot. (Honestly, I’m still trying to wrap my head around the optics of that room).

item.title: Trump Fires AG Bondi: Todd Blanche Named New Attorney General
item.description: If you feel like you need a spreadsheet just to keep track of who is running the U.S. government lately, you aren’t alone. On April 2, 2026, the political landscape shifted again when President Donald Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi after only 14 months in the job. Replacing her is Todd Blanche—a man already deeply embedded in both the President’s legal history and the most controversial chapters of this administration. Honestly, the turnover rate in this White House is enough to give anyone whiplash.
